licenses.name: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0
licenses.description: Allows sharing and adaptation for non-commercial purposes only.

metadata.explanation_hint: Designed for creative content, not software. Prohibits commercial use.

licenses.name: Creative Commons Zero v1.0 Universal
licenses.description: Public domain dedication with fallback permissive license for jurisdictions that don't recognize public domain waivers.

metadata.explanation_hint: Preferred public domain tool for datasets and creative works. More legally robust than Unlicense internationally. Not OSI-approved (CC licenses are not designed for software).

licenses.name: Common Development and Distribution License 1.0
licenses.description: File-level copyleft from Sun Microsystems; used for OpenSolaris and ZFS. Modifications to covered files must be shared.

metadata.explanation_hint: Historically used by Sun/Oracle for ZFS and NetBeans. INCOMPATIBLE with GPL (FSF says so explicitly) — major source of controversy when Linux wanted ZFS. Not recommended for new projects.

licenses.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
licenses.description: Allows any use including commercial, with attribution required. Designed for creative works, not software.

metadata.explanation_hint: For documentation, datasets, media assets. NOT recommended for source code. OSI explicitly states CC licenses are not suitable for software.

licenses.name: Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International
licenses.description: Attribution required; derivatives must use the same or a compatible license (ShareAlike = copyleft for content).

metadata.explanation_hint: Wikipedia's license. 'Copyleft for content' — derivatives must also be CC-BY-SA. Not for software. Compatible with GPL-3.0 per Creative Commons.

licenses.name: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International
licenses.description: Allows sharing and adaptation for non-commercial purposes only with attribution.

metadata.explanation_hint: For academic/creative work you want freely shared but not monetized. 'Commercial' is broadly interpreted — even indirect revenue can count. Not for software.

licenses.name: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International
licenses.description: Non-commercial use only; derivatives must use the same license. Most restrictive standard CC license.

metadata.explanation_hint: Widely used for educational materials and course content. Maximum control for non-commercial creators. Incompatible with most software licenses.
